en.extract:
The North Moluccan pitta (Erythropitta rufiventris) is a species of the pitta. It was considered a subspecies of the red-bellied pitta.  It is endemic to Indonesia where it occurs on the northern Moluccas.  Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.  It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''North Moluccan pitta''' ('''''Erythropitta rufiventris''''') is a species of the [[pitta]]. It was considered a subspecies of the [[red-bellied pitta]].  It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Indonesia]] where it occurs on the northern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]].  Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.  It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
